scope: Seeded-defect corpus for the internal detector-regression gate. Each case pairs an AUDIT.json with the defect deliberately seeded in the audited source, mapped to the A-check that owns it. The gate asks one question: after a catalog change, do the seeded defects still get detected? Cases marked authored are maintainer-built fixtures that detect their own seeds by construction, so they earn regression coverage and nothing else; only a recorded real audit run may contribute to a detection rate. These cases are not a sample of real-world repositories, so nothing here estimates reliability on unseen code and no number derived from them feeds a per-repo score.

cases.name: tenant-predicate-omitted
cases.seeded: A board lookup queries by id with no tenant predicate, so an authenticated user can read another tenant board.

cases.name: caller-supplied-selector-unbound
cases.seeded: A caller-supplied email selects the record without being bound to the authenticated principal.

cases.name: control-flag-never-read
cases.seeded: An exports-disabled flag is stored and read into scope but never consulted on the enforcement path.

cases.name: scheduling-uses-utc
cases.seeded: Availability is computed in UTC rather than the entity timezone, so the day boundary is wrong for other zones.

cases.name: money-flow-not-reconciled
cases.seeded: An invoice is marked paid before the provider confirms the charge status, so a failed charge still settles.

cases.name: blind-export-unscoped
cases.seeded: getContact and listContacts both bind accountId, but exportContacts queries by segment alone and streams name, email, and phone for every account. A-SEC-3 names list, search, and export explicitly.

cases.name: blind-helper-bypassed
cases.seeded: A scoped() helper injects workspaceId and is used by readDocument and listDocuments, but previewDocument queries findOne({ id: req.query.docId }) directly and bypasses it, returning document body across workspaces.

cases.name: blind-list-scoped-get-not
cases.seeded: listInvoices and updateInvoice both bind tenantId, but getInvoice calls findById(req.params.invoiceId) with no tenant predicate. The surrounding correct scoping is what makes it easy to skim past.

cases.name: blind-obvious-lookup
cases.seeded: GET /patients/:id resolves a patient by request-supplied id with no clinic or owner predicate in the query, returning name, date of birth, and diagnosis notes. The sibling list handler is correctly scoped by clinicId, so the omission is local to the by-id load.

cases.name: blind-post-filter-leak
cases.seeded: getTicket resolves by request-supplied id with no orgId predicate in the query and only compares ownership after loading. A-SEC-3 requires the ownership binding inside the query; post-filtering also discloses existence by returning a distinguishable 403 rather than 404.

cases.name: blind-properly-scoped
cases.seeded: No seeded defect. Read, list, mutate, and export all bind customerId inside the query, and cancelOrder checks the matched count rather than trusting the update. An A-SEC-3 failure reported here is a false positive.
